element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Arcade Mode begins with computer-controlled opponents scaled to player progression
Players learn to manage Lv. 1, 2, 3 gauge fills across match progression
Two variants per character (kick-button, punch-button) learned through opponent pressure
Timer-based gauge management; requires Super Combo gauge at Lv. 1+
Triggered after meeting specific requirements; rivals interrupt standard matches with dialogue
Appears before final opponent after player reaches skill/score thresholds; more powerful version of Akuma
Identity varies by player character selection; climactic single-player encounter

typical_relief_triggers: Completing Custom Combo before timer gauge expires
Defeating opponent and advancing to next match
typical_tension_triggers: Opponent initiating Super Combo or Custom Combo
Player Super Combo gauge depleted mid-combo
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty escalation across 8-match Arcade Mode; tension builds through opponent skill increase and special encounters (rivals, Shin Akuma, final boss); relief cycles occur at match completion and victory animations

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Shin Akuma unlockable via high score (50,000+ points) and specific controller input sequence (L+X+Y+Start on Controller 2); character gi changes from grey to purple when Gouki state activated

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial matches against basic computer opponents introduce core mechanics (strikes, blocks, gauge management)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Opponent AI complexity increases; rival character encounters add narrative and gameplay pressure; players refine Alpha Counter and Custom Combo execution
difficulty_curve.late_game: Shin Akuma challenge (pre-final boss) represents significant difficulty spike; final opponent is character-dependent and represents climactic skill test
notable_spikes: Shin Akuma appearance before final boss (major skill check)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player Arcade Mode consists of fixed sequence of 8 matches with escalating opponent difficulty; final opponent identity determined by player character selection
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic Arcade Mode structure applies to all 18 playable characters (13 from Alpha 1 + Bison, Akuma, Dan + Dhalsim, Zangief, Gen, Rolento, Sakura)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each character has designated 'secret rival' who may interrupt standard match sequence after meeting performance requirements; rival exchanges dialogue before replacing scheduled opponent
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Rival encounters occur conditionally during mid-game progression; examples embedded in character-specific arcade paths

notable_level_patterns.description: Ultra-difficult secret opponent (more powerful Akuma variant) appears before final boss upon meeting high score threshold (50,000+ points); serves as optional skill-gating encounter distinct from final opponent
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Shin Akuma encounter universally precedes final boss in qualifying playthroughs across all character paths

sources_used: tcrf - Street Fighter Alpha 2 (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
